last_gate.rationale: All four issue_hints from the first iteration are resolved with concrete plan amendments: Phase 0 audits touchpoints with explicit no-change rationale, Phase 4 provides a concrete shader-validation extraction strategy with timeline-domain mapping rules, Phase 2 adds projection filtering to prevent duplicate descriptor pollution, and the MaterialRef alias policy is documented with no M1a deprecation. No unresolved significant flags remain. Weighted score is 0 (clean). Preflight checks all passed. The 14 success criteria span must-verify behaviors across SDK exports, runtime index construction, duplicate handling, shader compatibility, registry hardening, and scope containment. The plan is thorough enough to execute.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Score trajectory: 5.0 (iteration 1, four blocking issue_hints) → 0 (iteration 2, all resolved). No recurring critiques. No scope creep flags. 4 resolved flags, 0 addressed-but-unverified, 0 unresolved. Plan delta 93.7% reflects substantial revision. Preflight posture clean: project dir exists and is writable, success criteria present, Claude and Codex available.
warnings: Criterion 14 (should priority) depends on subjective judgment of scope containment — verify during execution that no general composition graph contract, target-path model, material runtime, or shader stacks leak in.

The iteration pressure analysis shows FG-001 (MaterialRef alias) and FG-002 (criterion 13 subjective verification) as two-iteration recurring groups. FG-001 is fully resolved in the plan text; FG-002 is inherent to the 'should' priority criterion and will need reviewer attention during the review stage, not further plan revision.

settled_decisions.decision: Contribution identity defaults to kind:extensionId:contributionId; version and compatibility range are resolver inputs, not part of the default key.
settled_decisions.rationale: Keeps the key deterministic and stable for index lookups without baking version coupling into the identity layer. The plan explicitly reserves version fields as resolver-level concerns.

settled_decisions.decision: MaterialRef is a composition-facing alias for the existing RenderMaterialRef type; RenderMaterialRef remains public and supported with no M1a deprecation or migration.
settled_decisions.rationale: Avoids breaking existing consumers and avoids introducing a parallel material model. The alias provides SDK ergonomics for composition scenarios without forcing a rename.

settled_decisions.decision: Cross-extension bare contributionId reuse and same-extension cross-kind reuse are valid; only exact scoped-key duplicates (same kind, extensionId, contributionId) are diagnosed as warnings.
settled_decisions.rationale: Enables independent extension authors to use the same bare ID without coordination overhead while still catching accidental intra-extension duplicates.

settled_decisions.decision: Exact scoped-key duplicates are fully preserved in sequencing and the contribution index with structured resolutionPolicy metadata, but only the first occurrence reaches runtime descriptor arrays (projection filtering).
settled_decisions.rationale: Preserves auditability and diagnostics while preventing duplicate runtime descriptors from polluting render planner or UI consumers. First-wins is projection fallback only, not permanent semantic resolution.

settled_decisions.decision: Reserved graph patch operation families (graph.node.*, graph.edge.*, graph.group.*) and diff granularities (graph, node, edge) are known to SDK types and host patch validation but remain non-executable, non-previewable warnings.
settled_decisions.rationale: Reserves the vocabulary now so that when graph contracts are introduced in a future milestone, existing extension SDKs and patch validation can recognize and handle graph ops without a breaking vocabulary change.

settled_decisions.decision: Shader scope validation is extracted into a host composition helper that imports no timeline-domain issue types or render planner types; timeline-domain.ts maps the helper's result back to its existing TimelineDomainIssue/TimelineShaderAssignmentResult shapes.
settled_decisions.rationale: Centralizes the validation logic so renderPlanner.ts can reuse the same helper for planner shader diagnosis without coupling planner types to timeline-domain types or vice versa, while preserving test compatibility for existing error payloads.

settled_decisions.decision: No manifest-facing schema vocabulary is introduced in this milestone; config/contracts/reigh-extension.schema.json remains unchanged.
settled_decisions.rationale: This milestone changes runtime/reference semantics and governance fixtures, not the manifest schema vocabulary that extension authors declare against. Schema drift is explicitly verified in the validation phase.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize.
